report_name: Akamai Report: Financial Sector Under Siege from AI-Powered Botnets and Escalating DDoS Attacks

attack_patterns.name: Network Denial of Service
attack_patterns.description: Persistent and targeted Distributed Denial-of-Service (DDoS) campaigns are being launched against the financial sector, enhanced with AI to optimize traffic and evade mitigation.

attack_patterns.name: Exploit Public-Facing Application
attack_patterns.description: Threat actors are actively targeting and exploiting vulnerable, often poorly monitored, APIs for mobile and open banking to bypass traditional security perimeters.

attack_patterns.name: Phishing
attack_patterns.description: Used as a key component of Authorized Push Payment (APP) fraud, with AI being used to generate highly convincing phishing emails and text messages to manipulate victims.

attack_patterns.name: Cloud Infrastructure Discovery
attack_patterns.description: Attackers use automated, AI-powered tools to discover and map an organization's cloud and API footprint to identify targets.

attack_patterns.name: Credential Stuffing
attack_patterns.description: Attackers perform credential stuffing attacks against API authentication endpoints to gain unauthorized access.

attack_patterns.name: Generate Deepfakes
attack_patterns.description: AI is used to enhance social engineering for APP fraud schemes by generating deepfake voice calls to manipulate victims.

malware.name: AI-Empowered Botnet
malware.description: A new generation of botnets that use Artificial Intelligence to operate more autonomously, adapting their attack methods in real-time to bypass defenses and execute complex, multi-stage attacks at high speed and scale.

tools.name: Artificial Intelligence
tools.description: Adversaries are weaponizing Artificial Intelligence to enhance their attacks against the financial sector, including optimizing DDoS traffic, automating vulnerability discovery, and improving social engineering for fraud.

vulnerabilities.name: Broken Object Level Authorization
vulnerabilities.description: A class of API vulnerability actively exploited by attackers, where an API call is manipulated to access data for which the user should not be authorized. Mentioned as a common API attack vector.

identities.name: Financial Services Sector
identities.description: The primary target of a 'sustained siege' of sophisticated cyberattacks, including AI-empowered botnets and escalating DDoS campaigns, according to a report by Akamai.

infrastructure.name: AI-Empowered Botnet Infrastructure
infrastructure.description: Infrastructure for AI-driven botnets that can operate more autonomously and adapt their attack methods in real-time to bypass defenses.

indicators.name: High Rate of Application Layer DDoS Traffic
indicators.pattern:
title: High Rate of Application Layer DDoS Traffic
id: 2b9a1c8d-4e5f-4a6b-8c9d-0e1f2a3b4c5d
status: experimental
description: Detects a high rate of HTTP/S requests from a distributed set of source IPs targeting application-layer resources, indicative of an application-layer DDoS attack.
references:
  - https://cybermagazine.com/articles/this-weeks-top-five-stories-in-cyber
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-24
tags:
  - attack.t1498
level: high
logsource:
  category: webserver
detection:
  selection:
    c_source_ip:
      # This is a behavioral rule; specific IPs are not listed.
      # The logic should be implemented in a SIEM to count distinct IPs per destination over a time window.
      - 'any'
    c_uri_path|contains:
      - '/api/v1/payments'
      - '/login'
  timeframe: 1m
  condition: selection | count(c_source_ip) by c_uri_path > 100
indicators.description: Detects HTTP/S traffic with high request rates from a distributed set of IPs, targeting application-layer resources like login pages or API endpoints, as described in the Akamai report.

indicators.name: Potential API Credential Stuffing
indicators.pattern:
title: Potential API Credential Stuffing Attempt
id: f8a7b6c5-d4e3-4f2a-1b09-c8d7e6f5a4b3
status: experimental
description: Detects a rapid succession of login attempts for a single user account from multiple source IPs, targeting an API endpoint. This may indicate a credential stuffing attack.
references:
  - https://cybermagazine.com/articles/this-weeks-top-five-stories-in-cyber
author: cyber.netsecops.io
date: 2026-05-24
tags:
  - attack.t1110.004
level: medium
logsource:
  category: webserver
detection:
  selection:
    c_uri_path|contains:
      - '/api/'
    # Logic to detect many IPs for one user account over a short time.
    # This is a behavioral rule concept for a SIEM.
  timeframe: 5m
  condition: selection | count(c_source_ip) by user > 10
indicators.description: Detects a rapid succession of login attempts from multiple IPs for a single user account, a pattern indicative of credential stuffing against API endpoints.

relationships.source_name: High Rate of Application Layer DDoS Traffic
